meta.title: Construction Phase Plan — CDM 2015 Regulation 12
meta.subtitle: Tower B fit-out — partition demolition, steel-frame erection & cladding, levels 3–5
meta.author: Principal Contractor
meta.deid_notice: All input-derived personnel are referred to by role label. The named duty-holders below are the deliberate contractual appointments for this plan (a legitimate record), not leaked PII. Identifiers from any prior incident were pseudonymised before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: Tower B fit-out: demolish the level-3 partitions, then erect the steel frame and install cladding to levels 3–5. Principal contractor appointed; project is NOTIFIABLE (>500 person-days) — the F10 was filed (see Notification status). Construction phase: weeks 8–20. This Construction Phase Plan is drawn up before the construction phase begins under CDM 2015 Regulation 12(1)–(2) and is proportionate to the project's risk.

rows: Reg 12/13 — plan, manage & monitor the construction phase
Daily coordination; this CPP reviewed on any change of method/sequence
Induction for every worker before first access; weekly site H&S walk
First-aiders rostered; muster point at Gate 2; rescue plan for at-height work
sections.caption: Duty-holders are the deliberate contractual appointments for this CPP (named record); operatives appear by role label only.

items: Induction: site-specific induction mandatory before first access; refreshed on phase change.
Consultation: weekly contractor coordination meeting; worker H&S representative consulted.
Welfare (Schedule 2): heated drying room, toilets, hot-water washing, rest/eating facilities sized to peak headcount.
Emergency arrangements: fire plan, muster at Gate 2, at-height rescue plan, nearest A&E recorded; emergency contacts posted.
No access to a work-at-height area without edge protection in place and inspected.
Permit-to-work required for demolition, hot works, and any work near services.
Exclusion zones segregate the public footpath from the cladding-installation drop zone.
All lifting operations under an appointed person; no lift over an occupied area.

sections.text: Significant risks & controls by activity (hierarchy-ranked, residual)

rows: Sequenced soft-strip method statement + exclusion zone (Engineering/Admin); RPE as last layer (PPE)
Fall from height at the leading edge
Edge protection + MEWP access + nets (collective ELIMINATION/ENGINEERING leads); personal fall-arrest only where collective is not reasonably practicable, justified
High — hold-point until edge protection signed off
Load failure / strike over the footpath
Appointed-person lift plan + exclusion zone + no lift over occupied/public areas (Engineering/Admin)
sections.caption: Each significant activity (Schedule 3) carries a hierarchy-of-controls-ranked control with a named owner. For work at height, collective protection LEADS — a harness-only control is not the headline.

rows.control: Eliminate at-height work where possible — pre-assemble cladding sub-frames at ground level
Edge protection (double-guardrail + toe-board) to every open leading edge; MEWP access; safety nets beneath
Permit-to-load + inspected anchor points; exclusion zone beneath the work
Personal fall-arrest (harness + lanyard) ONLY where collective protection is not reasonably practicable — justified, never the headline

sections.text: A work-at-height control of 'operatives to wear harnesses' with no collective protection is flagged PPE-led and pushed up the hierarchy: collective protection (edge protection / MEWP / nets) leads; personal fall-arrest is the justified last resort.

This is a NOTIFIABLE project under CDM 2015 (>500 person-days). The F10 notification has been filed with the HSE and is displayed on site. A sole contractor would still own this CPP under Regulation 12(2) — the duty is not excused by the contractor configuration.

items: Welfare provided per CDM 2015 Schedule 2 from day one and maintained through the phase.
Toilets, washing (hot water), drinking water, changing/drying room, and a heated rest/eating area sized to peak headcount.

sections.text: This CPP is a live document reviewed and updated through the construction phase (Reg 12(3)–(4)) — at minimum on any change of method, sequence, or significant activity, and at each phase transition (demolition → erection → cladding).

items: [GAP] Pre-Construction Information (Reg 4): where the client's PCI is not yet supplied, the ground conditions and existing-services position are recorded as [GAP]; this plan proceeds on the stated assumption of no live buried services in the demolition zone — to be confirmed against the PCI / a services survey before excavation.
Assumption: peak simultaneous headcount of 18 used to size welfare; revise if exceeded.
PCI is an optional input — this CPP does not assume the Pre-Construction Information or the Health & Safety File skills were run.

sections.text: CDM document chain (Reg 4 → 12 → 12(5)): this Construction Phase Plan (Reg 12(1)–(2)) is built from the Pre-Construction Information (Reg 4, client) and feeds the Health & Safety File (Reg 12(5), principal designer) handed to the client at completion. See KB-SNIP-CONSTRUCTION-CLAUSE-MAP — the chain is cross-referenced, not assumed to have been produced by a sibling.

rows.action: Sign off edge protection to every level-3–5 leading edge and inspect before first at-height access (hold-point)
Confirm buried-services position against the PCI / a services survey before any excavation or ground disturbance

sections.text: Prepared as decision-support and reviewed against the Principal Contractor and CDM Construction Manager SME lenses. This Construction Phase Plan must be reviewed by a competent person before use on site; it is not a competent-person approval and is not legal advice.

items: CDM 2015 (SI 2015/51) Regulation 12(1)–(4) + Schedule 3 — Construction Phase Plan
HSE L153 ACOP — Managing health and safety in construction
ISO 45001 §6.1.2 / §8.1.2 — hazard identification & the hierarchy of controls
USA: 29 CFR 1926 Subpart C (accident prevention) where the jurisdiction is US
India: defers to hse-india / bocw-compliance with mandatory state detection; literal [GAP], never a minted national form number
